evidence_snippets: SRS outline (IEEE-flavored)
coverage_entries.reason: Meeting notes require IEEE-style SRS conformity.

evidence_snippets: SRS is for the advisor and committee only.
coverage_entries.reason: No statement about submission for MSE degree found.

evidence_snippets: The SRS describes framework behavior
coverage_entries.reason: Document explicitly described as describing framework behavior.

evidence_snippets: Document is for the advisor and committee only.
coverage_entries.reason: Meeting notes and emails require restricted visibility.

evidence_snippets: Constraints/assumptions to state explicitly in Sec 2
coverage_entries.reason: Sec 2 content and mapping are specified in meeting notes.

evidence_snippets: Use cases (sketches for Sec 3)
coverage_entries.reason: Meeting notes provide Sec 3 use case and requirements plan.

evidence_snippets: publish a join notice to a control channel
coverage_entries.reason: Join notifications are explicitly specified in multiple artifacts.

evidence_snippets: a corresponding leave notice
coverage_entries.reason: Leave notifications are explicitly specified in sources.

evidence_snippets: we stop delivering that group's messages to it
coverage_entries.reason: Leave/opt-out semantics state deliveries halt immediately.

evidence_snippets: A substitute agent can join and act within the same group
coverage_entries.reason: Agent substitution and takeover described in notes and transcript.

evidence_snippets: support sending and receiving unicast
coverage_entries.reason: Transcript and notes describe unicast send/receive capability.

evidence_snippets: Delivery goes only to the specified destination
coverage_entries.reason: Source states delivery is targeted to specified address only.

evidence_snippets: in-order delivery
coverage_entries.reason: Unicast is described as ordered (A then B arrives A then B).

evidence_snippets: You can toggle encryption per send
coverage_entries.reason: Per-message protection/encryption capability is described.

evidence_snippets: automatically presented as plaintext
coverage_entries.reason: Receivers handle protected payloads and present plaintext automatically.

evidence_snippets: sender choose encryption per message
coverage_entries.reason: Sources explicitly state agents can choose protection per send.

evidence_snippets: automatically presented as plaintext
coverage_entries.reason: Automatic decryption/handling of protected messages is described.

evidence_snippets: Multiple group subscriptions allowed concurrently
coverage_entries.reason: Documents describe multicast groups, subscriptions, and reception.

evidence_snippets: Explicit join request required
coverage_entries.reason: Join request required before receiving any group traffic.

evidence_snippets: Explicit leave request
coverage_entries.reason: Leave request stops deliveries; sources specify leave() semantics.

evidence_snippets: no group traffic is delivered to an agent
coverage_entries.reason: Source enforces no delivery until join request accepted.

evidence_snippets: we stop delivering that group’s messages to it
coverage_entries.reason: Deliveries stop immediately after the leave request is processed.

evidence_snippets: We expose TTL and socket options
coverage_entries.reason: TTL is exposed and configurable via APIs.

evidence_snippets: Expose TTL, group address, and port
coverage_entries.reason: Group address and port are configurable via config/APIs.

evidence_snippets: Multiple concurrent group subscriptions are supported
coverage_entries.reason: System supports binding to and receiving from multiple groups.

evidence_snippets: Optional group message protection when configured
coverage_entries.reason: Group-level protection (encrypt) is supported when configured.

evidence_snippets: handle protected messages automatically when configured
coverage_entries.reason: Recipients automatically handle (decrypt) protected group messages.

evidence_snippets: broadcast send and receive are supported
coverage_entries.reason: Framework supports sending broadcast messages on the LAN.

evidence_snippets: broadcast receive is supported
coverage_entries.reason: Framework supports receiving broadcast messages.

evidence_snippets: all hosts on the local subnet see it
coverage_entries.reason: Broadcasts are scoped to local subnet; reach all local hosts.

evidence_snippets: interoperate with agentMom 1.2
coverage_entries.reason: Sources state compatibility with agentMom 1.2.

evidence_snippets: must demo by end of Phase II
coverage_entries.reason: Starred driving capabilities committed to Phase II demo.

evidence_snippets: agent-driven conversations
coverage_entries.reason: Spec explicitly supports agent-driven conversation mode.

evidence_snippets: component-driven mode
coverage_entries.reason: Spec explicitly supports component-driven conversation orchestration.

unsupported_implied.text: The system shall tag unicast messages with sequence numbers (or equivalent) and expose those sequence numbers in logs so reordering can be detected and verified.
unsupported_implied.source_snippet: "tagging with sequence numbers so we can detect reordering" / "I’ll wire logs to show unicast sequence numbers"
unsupported_implied.reason: The artifacts explicitly state using sequence numbers and logging them to detect/verify reordering. The gold set requires ordered unicast delivery but does not require sequence-number tagging or logging for verification/diagnosis; this is a distinct, significant capability implied by the source.

unsupported_implied.text: The system shall produce logs (or equivalent observable events) that include group join and leave events so behavior can be demonstrated and audited.
unsupported_implied.source_snippet: "I’ll wire logs to show ... the join/leave events for groups" / "We emit group-level events. On join, we publish a join notice ... on leave, a corresponding leave notice."
unsupported_implied.reason: While the gold set requires notifications for join/leave, the artifacts additionally require that these events be recorded/exposed in logs for demonstration and QA. Logging/auditing of these events is a separate operational capability not covered by the gold requirements.

unsupported_implied.text: The system shall ensure protected (encrypted) messages are unusable by recipients that lack the required keying material or are not members of the protected group (i.e., non-members cannot make use of protected traffic).
unsupported_implied.source_snippet: "If the recipient lacks what’s needed, the payload won’t be usable by that recipient" / "Non-members shouldn’t be able to make use of that traffic."
unsupported_implied.reason: The artifacts state that protected messages must not be usable by recipients without the proper material (and non-members should not be able to use protected group traffic). The gold set specifies encryption/decryption capabilities but does not state this access-control behavior for recipients lacking keys or for non-members; this is a significant security behavior implied by the sources.
